## [00:00 - 03:05]

Okay guys, welcome to today's training where I'm going to take you through a live e-commerce campaign build. So the first step that we always do inside of the agency before we go and even touch the ad account or anything like that is we map out the actual funnel. So I like to use a tool called Lucidchart. This is an example of it. There's a lot of different tools out there. So there's really no right or wrong. And I'm not saying that's what you need at all. That's just what we like to use. So I'm just downloading the sheet and I'm going to pull it up and talk to you guys about what we're looking at before we go into the ad account and start to build the actual campaign. So the funnel that we're working with right now is an e-commerce drop shipping Shopify store. And the products sell anywhere between sort of $15 to $150. So quite a wide spectrum but still within what we would call low ticket items. So typically for these types of products the margins are a little bit small. So you want a slightly shorter funnel or you want something that's a little bit punchier more to the point than a typical e-commerce funnel or certainly something that is higher ticket in value. It's not a well known brand name as well. So our aim is really we need to make sure that we're driving extremely cheap clicks at the top of our funnel and that we are also converting at the top of our funnel. So our top of funnel is also generating a sales. And the aim is that if we can trigger view content and add to cart that we will essentially have a big audience to retarget with our DPA. So if we quickly look here you'll see really there's only two steps to this funnel. We've got two top of funnels which I'll explain to you in a minute. And then we've got our bottom of funnel. You could technically add another step here and I'll talk to you about that in a moment. But let's quickly go through our top of funnel. So we've got this one here where we're going to be running a conversion objective. And this one is actually a DPA. So you can now create dynamic product ads to broad audiences so cold audiences and that requires a different campaign objective. So it needs to be run as a separate campaign. So we're essentially targeting exactly the same people within these two campaigns. So one of them is going to be interests and then one of them is a lookalike. So that should just say lookalike. So what we want to make sure is that these two campaigns are actually not running at the same time or if they are one of the ad sets is pause while the other one is live so that we're not actually going into the auction process against ourselves essentially trying to beat or compete against ourselves. And that will if you do that that will usually call stalling in one of the campaigns.

## [03:05 - 06:09]

It will cause one of your campaigns to not perform as well or to not feed out to that audience as well. Or it could also cause the results to be a lot more expensive. And then for our broad audience DPA we're going to run a broad audience carousel. And our success metrics for this is we're aiming for a 30 cents cost per click and we need that in order to have a viable funnel. We also want to have a nice healthy click through rate for a cold audience. So we're aiming here for 1.2 percent click through rate. Now I could even go so far here and say we want our cold audience to convert at $40 CPA. So the maximum CPA that our cold audience could convert at is $40. And as long as our bottom of funnel converts very cheaply so under sort of $8 then the whole funnel will make sense. And then in the bottom of the fun on what we have here is we're simply actually going to recharge it those that triggered viewed content and added to cart in the last 14 days. We're going to include all our placements here and we're going to feed this out as daily. These ones are on lifetime if you'll notice. Now the reason we're doing lifetime is there's not much of a difference guys and really it's more of a convenience aspect. But Facebook certainly seems to show a bit more preference towards lifetime budgets because they're evergreen in nature at the very root of them. However, what I would say here is that they're harder to scale. So the difference is almost negligible. However, we're going to set this up as lifetime but our retargeting is going to be on daily at $15. And we're going to run this as a DPA carousel. And again, we have our success metrics here. So we could technically have a separate campaign that is exclusively targeting our video viewers and excluding those that actually triggered the view content. So anyone that watched the video but didn't click and trying to drive them through to the website with a different ad. So that is definitely another step I would highly encourage you to run. Likewise, this is only retargeting 14 days. So I would highly recommend that you also include more stacks, retargeting stacks in your own campaign so that you retarget people say last 30 days, retarget people last 60

## [06:09 - 09:07]

days, etc., etc. And exclude one audience from the other so that you're not cannibalizing yourself again. And finally, the last thing that I want to add here is I haven't made this super clear here. However, what we're seeing now is that Facebook really loves it when your top of funnel ads are being shown to those that purchased as well. It helps with your relevance and all of that other good stuff. And I think more than anything, your customers will start to comment saying, hey, I just got mine. I love it. It looks awesome. And that is pretty powerful psychological social proof for a cold audience to see. It doesn't get much better than that. And that's why we think it works so well.
